title: niphobolus scolopendrium
intro: Niphobolus scolopendrium, also known as the maidenhair spleenwort, is a species of fern native to tropical and subtropical regions of Asia and the Pacific. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal effects. The health benefits of Niphobolus scolopendrium include its ability to lower blood pressure, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. The plant is typically prepared as a tea or infusion, or used in herbal remedies such as tinctures and ointments, to harness its medicinal properties.
